content: Node.js EventEmitter: required modules are singletons by default, so exporting 'new EventEmitter()' from a single module (e.g. src/events/domainEvents.js) gives every requirer the same shared bus instance -- no extra library needed. Listeners run synchronously in registration order; async listeners (async function handlers) can throw unhandled promise rejections if not wrapped in try/catch, since EventEmitter does not await listener promises. If an 'error' event is emitted with no 'error' listener registered, Node treats it as an uncaught exception and crashes the process -- always register a top-level .on('error', ...) handler on any shared EventEmitter.
